text:
Yeah whoever builds the AI agent that curates the entire internet for you and presents you with content that is most likely to make your life better is going to make a lot of money. 

The algorithms are so strong, so potent, the only way to counteract it is an AI that acts in your self interest. 

There’s so much value on the internet. 
But it’s going to get harder to find.

I want an AI to mediate all the content I watch. Not Elons AI, not Marks, not Sundars, not Satyas, mine. 

I will invest in someone trying to crack this.
author: Riley Brown

text:
you can use old forums from 2012-2018 to farm insane ai app ideas…

back then people would post these insanely ambitious app concepts that were basically impossible to build without a dev team and $50k (minum)

now you can make most of them in an afternoon with claude code

ask chatgpt to find you lists of old app idea forums, blogs, from that era - stuff like discussions, random wordpress blogs dedicated to "million dollar app ideas"

then use google dorking to actually dig through them (have gpt write the search queries for you)

you'll find hundreds of posts where people described genuinely interesting concepts but couldn't execute bc the tech wasn't there or they didn't have the skills

the ideas aren't the bottleneck anymore. we had them 10 years ago. execution is just way easier now

found 3 solid concepts yesterday that would've taken a team months to build in 2015, now it's a weekend project

stop trying to think of "original" ideas and just go mine the ones people already validated wanting years ago

recreate this with my own alo yoga pilates style influencer model with X product in hand (upload your product image as well)

author: Penelope Lopez

text:
Nano Banana + Fastmoss + Manus + Veo3 = AI Content Factory

No actors.
No products in hand.
No ghost creators.
No missed deadlines.
Just viral TikTok Shop sales — 24/7.

Here’s the crazy part:

This system produces 300+ cinematic, product-ready ads per day from a single prompt.

And it feels exactly like running Facebook Ads in 2008 — except the CPMs are even lower, and the entire loop is organic.

Here’s how the AI Creator Agent System works 

Each Agent runs its own TikTok Shop profile and handles an entire growth function:

• Trend + angle research using Kalodata
• Competitor ad cloning (paste their ad → pick an avatar → regenerate)
• Automated creator outreach with Euka AI
• Daily content generation using Sora 2 Pro, Veo3.1, HeyGen, RunwayML, Kling, Hedra
• Localization, repurposing, and multi-format output
• Compliance cleanup + optimization
• Automatic posting across a Multi-Platform Swarm (hundreds of agents)

No touchpoints.
No delays.
No human bottlenecks.

Just a decentralized force of AI + UGC creators selling while you sleep.

results:

• $0.10 CPMs
• Thousands of organic views daily 

This is the Creator Agent Method: a plug-and-play system that replaces entire creative teams and launches content at a speed humans simply can’t compete with.

I packaged my full AI Agent V3 workflow so you can deploy the exact system for your brand.

Comment AGENT and I’ll DM you everything for free.

(Deleting soon)

P.S. Repost for early access to the complete stack

This might be one of the BEST ways to ride the AI wave right now.

Build a mobile app using an AI app builder.
Create an AI influencer.
Post organic content on IG and TikTok.

I’ve been talking to people who are actually doing this, and the barrier to entry is way lower than you’d expect.

You don’t need to know how to code.
You don’t need to be on camera.
You don’t need an audience to start.

author: Prajwal Tomar

text:
before a pitch, i ask the prospect: "honestly, what’s the one thing you’re terrified of happening if you hire me?" 

they give me their deepest fear (wasted money, bad leads, ghosting). 

i then open a claude chat, dictate a clause that specifically penalizes ME if that exact fear happens, and add it to the contract live on screen. "there. i legally can't do that now." trust went from 20% to 100% in 10 seconds.

they can't sue me anyway. I have no residency anywhere

text:
every public company files a 10-K with the SEC once a year.

there's a section called "Risk Factors" where they legally have to disclose what keeps them up at night. 

cybersecurity threats. supply chain issues. regulatory concerns. it's all there.

ctrl+F whatever you sell. "data security" or "compliance" or "operational efficiency."

you now have a list of enterprise accounts that literally told the government they need what you're offering.

EDGAR is free. this takes 20 minutes. nobody does this.
